[[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] '''Olaver Lansel''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Imperial officer/Legends|Imperial officer]] serving the [[Moff Sarne's forces|military forces]] of [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Kentor Sarne]], the [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] of the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. A [[Sleeper agent/Legends|sleeper agent]], Lansel was inserted onto the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Gandle Ott]], posing as an [[Alcohol/Legends|alcoholic]] [[Bureaucrat/Legends|bureaucrat]] who had been punished for falling out with those loyal to Moff Sarne. Assigned as the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Imperial Liaison|liaison officer]], he was known to be vocally critical of the warlord's regime. In [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], Lansel was activated by Sarne when the Moff was forced to [[Battle of Kal'Shebbol|retreat]] from his [[Capital/Legends|capital]] on [[Kal'Shebbol/Legends|Kal'Shebbol]] in the wake of a [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] assault. Stopping at Gandle Ott, he gave Lansel orders to delay any New Republic pursuit, and to redirect them to the [[Shintel system/Legends|Shintel system]] where the Moff had an [[Skirmish at Shintel|ambush]] waiting. Shortly after, the New Republic [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] ''[[FarStar]]'' arrived at Gandle Ott. In a meeting with members of the [[Starship/Legends|starship's]] command crew, he directed them toward [[Shintel]] as Sarne had ordered.

==Biography==
[[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] Olaver Lansel was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who was part of the [[Moff Sarne's forces|forces]] of [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Kentor Sarne]] of the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]]. He served the Moff as a [[Sleeper agent/Legends|sleeper agent]], and was inserted onto [[Gandle Ott]] as the [[Planet/Legends|planet's]] Imperial [[Imperial Liaison|liaison officer]]. As part of his cover story, Lansel was known to have had a public falling out with [[Officer/Legends|officers]] personally loyal to Sarne, and had been assigned to Gandle Ott as punishment. Lansel was frequently seen to be [[Alcohol/Legends|drunk]] and was vocally critical of Sarne and his regime.<ref name="dirp" />

[[File:KentorSarne.png|thumb|right|240px|Olaver Lansel served under Kentor Sarne in the Kathol sector.]]
In [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]],<ref name="Date">{{DarkStryder|adventure=Death Is Remotely Possible|book=The DarkStryder Campaign}} dates itself to four [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], which equates to [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]] per ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> Moff Sarne was [[Battle of Kal'Shebbol|ousted]] from his [[Capital/Legends|capital]] on [[Kal'Shebbol/Legends|Kal'Shebbol]] by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. Falling back to Gandle Ott with his [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]], Sarne claimed to the population that he was embarking on a mission to purge the [[Sector/Legends|sector]] of [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebels]] and [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]]. Sarne took on supplies from the planet, recalled all loyal Imperial personnel—including the [[Unidentified Imperial Governor (Gandle Ott)|governor]]—and left [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] with the majority of the [[Gandle Ott system defense force|defense force]]. Before departing, Sarne activated Lansel, giving him orders to run the command files that the Moff had installed in the planetary [[BRT supercomputer/Legends|BRT supercomputer]], [[CUTH-BRT-92-X3]]. The programming would force CUTH-BRT-92-X3 to obey the orders of its Imperial controllers, which in that case was to ensure that the ''[[FarStar]]'', a New Republic [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] in [[DarkStryder Campaign|pursuit]] of the Moff, was delayed and inconvenienced as much as possible. Lansel was also given orders to slow down any New Republic pursuit by redirecting them to the neighboring world of [[Shintel]], where Sarne had arranged a [[Skirmish at Shintel|trap]] for the [[Corvette/Legends|corvette]].<ref name="dirp" />

Several [[Standard day/Legends|days]] after Sarne's departure, the ''FarStar'' arrived in orbit and landed at [[Montrol Spaceport]]. Lansel was part of a delegation led by [[Marja Lang]], the planet's Acting-[[Governor/Legends|Governor]] in the wake of her superior's departure, that greeted the crew. On the fourth day of the ''FarStar''{{'s}} visit, Lansel arranged for a brunch to be held at [[Torqumada Hanging Gardens]] to which the New Republic delegation was invited, along with various [[Media/Legends|media]] personalities, business leaders, and [[Politics/Legends|politicians]]. During the event, Lansel relayed the information regarding Shintel to members of the ''FarStar''{{'s}} crew, implying that he had overheard other [[Imperial officer/Legends|Imperial officers]] mentioning that Sarne was going to Shintel to "pick up the backups." With a supposedly solid lead on Sarne's location, the ''FarStar'' left the planet in pursuit after establishing [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomatic]] ties between the New Republic and Gandle Ott.<ref name="dirp" />

==Personality and traits==
Olaver Lansel was a pale, thin-faced man with mousy [[Color/Legends|brown]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]] and a mustache. Serious and polite, he was possessed of a dry sense of humor.<ref name="dirp" />

==Skills and abilities==
An undercover agent, Lansel was convincing at portraying a downtrodden and alcoholic [[Bureaucrat/Legends|bureaucrat]]. He was trained in the use of a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] and knowledgeable about the workings of a bureaucracy, could operate security systems, program [[Droid/Legends|droids]], and handle [[Bomb/Legends|explosives]].<ref name="dirp" />

==Equipment==
Lansel carried a [[Sporting blaster/Legends|sporting]] model blaster in addition to a [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]] about his person.<ref name="dirp" />

==Behind the scenes==
Olaver Lansel appeared in ''[[Death Is Remotely Possible]]'', the third [[Roleplaying game|RPG]] adventure in ''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]'' published in [[1995]] by [[West End Games]].<ref name="dirp" />
